text: The surgeon uses monopolar forceps to open the peritoneum of the mesentery just inferior to the ileocolic vessels, following the identification of the junction between the ileocolic and superior mesenteric vessels.
The surgeon identifies the junction between the ileocolic and superior mesenteric vessels and opens the peritoneum of the mesentery just inferior to the ileocolic vessels using monopolar forceps.
The surgeon develops the avascular plane between the mesocolon and the retroperitoneum using blunt dissection, visibly separating these tissue layers without causing bleeding.
